reasoning: Tool returns 386 hanabi festivals — correctly on-topic. However most items lack English name translations, descriptions, and prefecture info; many have null coordinates, prefecture, and description. An agent can enumerate options but cannot provide actionable details from the raw data alone. The response is voluminous but thin on usable content.
missing_data: English names for most entries; dates/season info; prefecture for majority of items; coordinates for most; no venue capacity or viewing tips.
improvement_hint: Add English translations for festival names via the existing translation pipeline; populate prefecture_code and coordinates from Wikidata where available; include typical month/season as a field.

reasoning: Same dataset as L3-01 returned in Japanese lang. 386 results is comprehensive but the data quality is the same: most items lack prefecture, coordinates, and descriptions. Japanese-language user gets same usability gap — names are Japanese but no actionable planning info.
missing_data: Prefecture and coordinates for most entries; season/date metadata; venue descriptions.
improvement_hint: Enrich Wikidata-sourced hanabi entries with prefecture codes and typical holding month; deduplicate and surface top-prominence items first.

reasoning: Query is about snow festivals in Japan (Hokkaido); tool returns count:0 for festivals in Hokkaido and falls back to national UNESCO ICH list. The fallback items (Dainichido Bugaku in Akita, Hayachine Kagura in Iwate, Kawagoe Hikawa Festival, Tatemon Festival in Toyama, Ueno Tenjin Festival in Mie) are irrelevant to snow festivals and mostly not even in Hokkaido. Sapporo Snow Festival is not returned at all.
missing_data: Sapporo Snow Festival; other Hokkaido winter festivals; festival season/month filters.
wrong_data: The returned national_heritage items are unrelated to snow festivals and are not in Hokkaido except Dainichido Bugaku (Akita).
improvement_hint: Add snow-festival / yukimatsuri keyword entries to the festivals corpus; ensure get_festivals resolves 'Hokkaido' string to prefecture_code '01' correctly and populates winter festival data.

reasoning: Query is about experiencing rural life (田舎暮らし体験) in Japanese villages; tool called is get_japan_heritage which returns heritage sites not rural life experience programs. Top items (Nagoya Castle, Sannai-Maruyama Site, archaeological ruins) are not rural life stays. The reasoning to use Japan Heritage for this intent is misaligned — the correct approach would be search_area with terms like 農家, 農泊, 里山.
missing_data: Nouhaku (農泊) stay programs; satoyama/village experience listings; craft villages; local rural townships.
improvement_hint: Index nōhaku (農泊) and satoyama experience content; route rural-life queries to search_area/search_hybrid rather than get_japan_heritage.

reasoning: Korean-language query about autumn foliage; search_area returns 147 matches including named venues (parks, gardens, castles with sakura_meisho_100/kinds tags). Top results are on-topic, contain coordinates, prefecture info, and heritage designations. Weak on English/Korean translations for most entries, and foliage timing/season data is absent. Reasonably useful as a discovery list.
missing_data: Korean name translations; autumn foliage timing (peak season months per region); specific maple/kouyou viewpoints beyond name-literal matches.
improvement_hint: Add kouyou-specific kind tags; include seasonal best-month metadata; ensure Korean name field is populated for top results.

reasoning: Query asks for quiet meditation places 'not a touristy temple'. Tool searches '修行' and returns temples and a ninja experience event (Akamedaki ninja training) and Rinzai-ji special viewing event. The explicit 'not touristy' and 'quiet' constraints are not addressed — some results (ninja training in Mie, busy Kyoto temples) run counter to the intent. The shugyo-ji in Tokyo is a city temple. There is no filtering or sorting for solitude/quietness.
missing_data: Zen retreat temples offering zazen; mountain temples with overnight stays; remote pilgrimage sites without tourist crowds.
improvement_hint: Add a 'remote' or 'religious_retreat' kind tag to temple entries; allow search_area to surface entries with shukubo/zazen metadata; flag urban vs. rural placement.

reasoning: Query in Spanish asks to see auroras in Japan. Auroras are extremely rare in Japan (only visible in Hokkaido during strong solar storms). The response returns observatories, national parks (Kushiro, Yanbaru, Iriomote-Ishigaki), a ropeway — all via kinds_class_match on observatory/park. None are aurora-watching spots. An agent synthesising this would give misleading advice about aurora tourism in Japan.
missing_data: Honest note that auroras are rare/unpredictable in Japan; Hokkaido sky-viewing spots where aurora sightings occasionally occur; alternative night-sky experiences.
wrong_data: Results suggest these venues are aurora destinations; they are not.
improvement_hint: Add a factual note in the response when query maps to phenomena that are exceedingly rare in Japan (aurora, midnight sun); return sky-observation venues with a caveat rather than implying aurora tourism.

reasoning: Query asks for 100-year-old houses (古民家) to sleep in; tool returns Gifu hotels — mostly 秘湯 onsen ryokan and OSM-sourced generic accommodations. None are labelled or verified as historic machiya or kominka. The intent is specifically heritage accommodation; results are generic Gifu hotels with no age/heritage attribute. 湯元すぎ嶋 and 槍見館 are remote onsen ryokan, not kominka.
missing_data: Kominka stay (古民家宿) listings; machiya accommodation in Takayama or Shirakawa-go; heritage designation for lodging type.
improvement_hint: Add a kominka/machiya lodging type to the hotel taxonomy; include heritage year or construction period where available from official sources; index HFE-designated traditional inns.

reasoning: Thai query asks for lesser-known cherry blossom spots away from tourists. search_area(桜) returns 201 results including sakura_meisho_100 and notable castles — which are actually the most famous spots (Himeji Castle, Kumamoto Castle). The 'hidden/non-touristy' constraint is completely ignored. Good geographic coverage; names mostly in Japanese. However the response is raw data, not ranked by obscurity.
missing_data: Lower-prominence sakura venues; non-meisho local park entries; countryside river embankment sakura spots.
improvement_hint: Add a 'prominence_score' filter option to de-rank famous spots when the intent signals 'off the beaten path'; expose lower-prominence results more prominently for such queries.

reasoning: Photography / dramatic landscape query; search_area(絶景) returns 53 results including specific named spots (Ehime scenic drives list, Kumamoto 360-degree shrine, Niigata scenery list). Municipal scrape spots include good descriptions in Japanese with named sub-spots. DMO content is specific. Limited English in descriptions but the data is substantive and covers multiple regions.
missing_data: Coordinates for some DMO-source spots; English names/descriptions for photography-centric venues.
improvement_hint: Add coordinates to DMO-scraped spots where page has map data; add an English summary for top spots to assist English-language agent synthesis.

reasoning: Arabic query asks for outdoor hot springs in nature, NOT in hotels. search_area(露天風呂) returns 72 results, many of which are hotel/resort onsen facilities (見奈良天然温泉利楽 is a resort, 小薮温泉 is a lodging). The 'not in hotels' constraint is not addressed. Good coverage of onsen spots with descriptions; 宝泉坊温泉 is close to natural, but most results are hotel-attached facilities.
missing_data: Wild/public natural outdoor onsen without lodging requirement; rotenburo accessible as day-trip without hotel booking.
improvement_hint: Add a 'rotenburo_public' vs 'hotel_attached' attribute to onsen spots; enable constraint filtering on 'no accommodation required'.

reasoning: Query asks about swimming with wild dolphins. search_area(イルカ) returns irrelevant results: first result is a Kyoto cultural ambassador page, second is an aquarium (Noto-jima Suizokukan), third is a fish market. The actual wild dolphin swim location (Mikurajima in Tokyo, or Amami/Okinawa) is not in results. Intent is completely missed; top results are noise or captive-dolphin aquariums.
missing_data: Mikurajima dolphin swim tours; Amami/Okinawa wild dolphin encounters; eco-marine tourism operators.
improvement_hint: Index dolphin-swimming experience operators from official tourism pages of Mikurajima, Ogasawara, and Amami; add 'marine_wildlife_experience' kind tag for such venues.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional architecture preservation districts (重伝建). get_japan_heritage is called but returns Japan Heritage (日本遺産) program — a different program. Top_canonical_heritage shows Nagoya Castle, Sannai-Maruyama archaeological site — neither is a preserved townscape district. The tool_scope_note itself warns this tool is not for general famous landmarks. The correct tool would be search_area with 重伝建 or preservation_district kind.
missing_data: Judenken (重要伝統的建造物群保存地区) districts like Gion, Shirakawa-go, Tsumago-juku, Uchiko.
improvement_hint: Route 'traditional architecture / preservation district' intent to search_area with preservation_district kind filter, or add get_preservation_districts tool; ensure Japan Heritage vs Judenken distinction is reflected in routing.

reasoning: Vietnamese query about Ainu culture in Hokkaido; search_area(アイヌ) returns 125 results with top hits being exactly right: Ainu Museum (Porotokotan), National Ainu Museum (Upopoy), Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum, Hokkaido Ainu Center, Kawamura Kaneto Ainu Museum — all with coordinates and in Hokkaido. Excellent coverage of the specific cultural topic. Missing Vietnamese translations but entity data is solid.
missing_data: Vietnamese name translations; Upopoy (ウポポイ) entry may be the older Porotokotan — slight entity consolidation question.
improvement_hint: Add Vietnamese translations for top Ainu-related entries; clarify Porotokotan vs. Upopoy entity relationship (old museum vs. new national complex at same site).

reasoning: Query about cliff-carved Buddhist statues (磨崖仏); search_area returns 72 matches with top results being excellent: Usuki Stone Buddhas (Oita, National Treasure, prominence 0.9), Oya-dera Magaibutsu (Tochigi, prominence 0.9), Kiyomizu Magaibutsu (Kagoshima). Coordinates, heritage designations present. Solid geographic spread across Kyushu and Kanto. Some entries lack coordinates or English names but the top-ranked ones are accurate and specific.
missing_data: English name/description for some secondary entries; a few entries have null coordinates.
improvement_hint: Add English descriptions for magaibutsu entries lacking them; ensure Oya-dera magaibutsu name_en reflects the statue complex not the temple alone.

reasoning: Query specifies day-trip volcano hikes from Tokyo. search_area(火山) returns volcanoes but ignores the Tokyo/day-trip constraint: top results include Mount Iwate (Iwate, >5h from Tokyo), Lake Towada (Aomori), Mount Unzen (Nagasaki), Lake Mashu (Hokkaido) — all impossible for a day trip from Tokyo. Mount Fuji is present and feasible. No feasibility filtering or proximity-to-Tokyo sorting is applied.
missing_data: Mount Fuji day-trip details; Mount Takao (accessible, not volcanic but alternative); Hakone volcanic landscape (Owakudani, day-trip viable); distance/travel time from Tokyo.
improvement_hint: Implement geographic proximity sorting when query specifies a base city; tag volcanoes with accessibility tiers from major hubs; surface day-trip feasibility metadata.

reasoning: Indonesian query: want to eat freshly-made tofu at a temple. search_area(豆腐) returns 84 results — GI-certified frozen tofu products (Iwadeyama kori-dofu), island tofu descriptions (Iki-shima), mountain tofu shops — but no temple-tofu (shojin ryori / tofu kaiseki at temples like Nanzen-ji, Daitoku-ji, Kofuku-in). The temple dimension of the query is completely missed; results are generic tofu food items.
missing_data: Shojin ryori (精進料理) temple restaurants; Kyoto tofu kaiseki at temple complexes; tofu-making workshops at temples.
improvement_hint: Add shojin ryori and temple food experience content; index DMO pages about yudofu (湯豆腐) in Kyoto; add 'temple_cuisine' kind tag.

reasoning: Query about fishing villages 'frozen in time'. search_area(漁港) returns fishing port results including Itoman fishing port (Okinawa), Teradomari fish market street (Niigata), a static/off-limits beach notice. The 'frozen in time' / historic preservation dimension is not captured — none of these are identified as heritage-listed traditional fishing villages. Good geographic spread but content does not address the nostalgic/heritage constraint.
missing_data: Hagi, Tomonoura, Ine no Funaya (boat houses) as UNESCO/nationally-designated preserved fishing villages.
improvement_hint: Add preservation_district kind to heritage fishing villages like Ine-cho funaya; surface result from Japan Heritage stories covering coastal fishing communities.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about washi (和紙) papermaking from a master craftsperson. get_local_specialty(craft) returns 231 traditional craft items — but starting with Nibutani Attus (Ainu textile), Oitama Tsumugi (silk weaving), Ugo Shina Cloth. Washi is in the corpus (likely further down, not shown in truncated view). The 'learn from a master' / workshop experience component is absent — the tool returns product designations, not experience venues.
missing_data: Washi papermaking workshop venues; specific artisan or studio visit information; Echizen, Mino, Tosa washi craftspeople.
improvement_hint: Add 'craft_experience_available' flag to METI densan entries that have associated workshop programs; link to official artisan experience programs from prefectural tourism pages.

reasoning: Japanese query about period-drama scenery (時代劇ロケ地); search_area(宿場) returns 104 matches with exactly right results at top: Narai-juku, Seki-juku, Tsumago-juku — all with preservation_district + shukuba kinds and Important Preservation District designations. Coordinates present, descriptions include walking and historical atmosphere content from municipal scrapes. Excellent intent alignment. Very good specificity for this niche query.
missing_data: Kyoto film set locations; Toei Uzumasa Eiga-mura; additional post-town descriptions in English.
improvement_hint: Add jidaigeki (time-period drama) film-set kind tag to relevant Kyoto-area attractions; surface Uzumasa Eiga-mura explicitly for film tourism queries.

reasoning: Query for uncrowded beaches. search_area(ビーチ) returns 115 results — top entries include Naminoue Beach in Naha (urban, well-known), multiple Okinawa resort beaches. The 'not crowded with tourists' constraint is ignored; the most touristed beaches in Okinawa are prominent. Geographic diversity exists but sorting does not reflect obscurity/crowd level.
missing_data: Low-traffic rural coastal spots; northern Japan beaches; off-island beaches with lower tourist footfall.
improvement_hint: Use prominence_score inversely when query contains 'uncrowded/not crowded' intent signals; surface lower-prominence beach entries; add beach access type (resort vs. public natural beach) attribute.

reasoning: Query about old-school izakaya/yokocho to drink with locals. search_area(横丁) returns 74 results with some good yokocho-kind entries (Haikara't Yokocho Shimonoseki, Okachimachi Ramen Yokocho). However top result is 横丁公園 (a park in Shinagawa, not a drinking alley), and second result is a generic Saga tourism page in Chinese that is clearly irrelevant noise. Actual famous yokocho (Shinjuku Golden Gai, Yurakucho, Ebisu) may be buried lower.
missing_data: Golden Gai Shinjuku; Yurakucho izakaya alley; Hoppy Street Asakusa — canonical drinking alleys.
improvement_hint: Add explicit yokocho kind to Golden Gai and major Tokyo/Osaka drinking alleys; filter out irrelevant page-level scrape noise (generic tourism portal pages in wrong language) from top results.

reasoning: German query about scenic local train travel through rural Japan. search_area(ローカル線) returns 74 results including Sagano Scenic Line (Kyoto), Oigawa Main Line (Shizuoka with steam trains), Oga Line (Akita), Oshu Main Line. Good mix of scenic/tourist railways with a Nagano editorial article about local train + art travel. Results have local_railway kind tags. Decent English names for notable lines, though German translations absent.
missing_data: German name translations; travel time/connection info from major hubs; key sightseeing points along each line.
improvement_hint: Add German translations for top scenic railway entries; include typical journey duration and key boarding station in metadata.

reasoning: Query about firefly viewing in Japan in summer. search_area(ホタル) returns 71 results with relevant hits: ホタルの里 (Hyogo park), Toyoda Firefly Museum (Yamaguchi, Japan's first firefly river cruise), Kitakyushu firefly viewing, Kanazawa Kenroku-en firefly viewing event with specific dates (6/26-7/4 2026). Good specificity and seasonal information in descriptions. Minor: some results are aquarium/museum entries not actual viewing sites.
missing_data: Specific best-season window (late May to June for genji-botaru); firefly species information; rural vs. urban viewing quality comparisons.
improvement_hint: Add best-season metadata to firefly spots; add firefly-viewing event kind tag; ensure hotaru_viewing distinguishes river/natural sites from aquarium exhibits.

reasoning: Russian query about watching cranes in Japan in winter. search_area(鶴) hits 200 results; top attraction hit is perfect: Izumi crane migration grounds (Kagoshima) with description, coordinates, crane_wintering kind, Special Natural Monument and Ramsar designations. Kushiro Shitsugen (Hokkaido, red-crowned crane habitat) is also returned. Municipality noise (Tsuruda-cho, Tsurui-mura etc.) is expected but the key wildlife sites are prominent and accurate.
missing_data: Tsurui-mura (Hokkaido, танчо鶴) explicit entry; winter crane season months (Oct-Mar for Izumi); visitor access info.
improvement_hint: Boost crane_wintering kind entities to top of results for wildlife-viewing queries; add season/month metadata to wildlife attraction entries; add Tsurui-iza Tancho Sanctuary as explicit entry.
